pathways.name: GABA synthesis, release, reuptake and degradation
pathways.top_level_parent: Neuronal System → Transmission across Chemical Synapses → Neurotransmitter release cycle
pathways.short_summary: Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) is a major inhibitory neurotransmitter. It is synthesised in presynaptic neurons, loaded into synaptic vesicles, released into the synaptic cleft and then recaptured by transporters or taken up by astrocytes for metabolism.

pathways.name: Tryptophan catabolism
pathways.top_level_parent: Metabolism → Metabolism of amino acids and derivatives
pathways.short_summary: Tryptophan is catabolised in seven steps to yield aminomuconate; intermediates in this process are also used to synthesise serotonin and kynurenine.

pathways.name: Serotonin and melatonin biosynthesis
pathways.top_level_parent: Metabolism → Metabolism of amino acids and derivatives → Metabolism of amine-derived hormones
pathways.short_summary: Serotonin (5-HT) is a hormone and neurotransmitter involved in many physiological functions and serves as a precursor for melatonin. It is synthesised from tryptophan and can be further converted to melatonin.

pathways.name: Metabolism of serotonin
pathways.top_level_parent: Neuronal System → Transmission across Chemical Synapses → Neurotransmitter clearance → Serotonin clearance from the synaptic cleft
pathways.short_summary: Serotonin released into the synaptic cleft is metabolised to 5-hydroxyindole acetaldehyde by monoamine oxidase and then to 5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid by aldehyde dehydrogenase.

pathways.name: Serotonin receptors
pathways.top_level_parent: Signal Transduction → Signaling by GPCR → GPCR ligand binding → Class A/1 (Rhodopsin-like receptors) → Amine ligand-binding receptors
pathways.short_summary: Serotonin (5-hydroxytryptamine) modulates multiple physiological functions such as mood and appetite. Seven families of serotonin receptors exist; all except the 5-HT3 family are G protein‑coupled receptors (GPCRs).

pathways.name: Toll-like receptor 3 (TLR3) cascade
pathways.top_level_parent: Immune System → Innate Immune System → Toll-like Receptor Cascades
pathways.short_summary: Toll‑like receptor 3 (TLR3) is expressed in dendritic cells and intestinal cells. It recognises double‑stranded RNA, signalling through the adaptor TRIF to activate NF‑kappa‑B and induce type I interferon genes.
